evaluation_note: Re-evaluation with updated top-3 from Scout composite ranking. Replaces Stochastic Resetting (rank #5) with Anesthetic Lipid Raft (rank #3).

evaluations.title: Earthquake ETAS Self-Exciting Point Process Models Tumor Recurrence Dynamics
evaluations.field_c: Clinical oncology — tumor dormancy and recurrence timing
concerns: CRITICAL: Self-exciting assumption requires metastatic events to genuinely trigger subsequent events. If tumor recurrence events are predominantly INDEPENDENT (driven by intrinsic dormancy exit clocks rather than inter-event triggering), the self-exciting kernel K collapses to ~0, reducing ETAS to an inhomogeneous Poisson process and making seismology-specific features irrelevant. Mitigation: K > 0 is itself a testable hypothesis; null finding is still informative.

MODERATE: Hawkes processes (parent class of ETAS) already applied to neural spike trains, multicellular signaling (PNAS 2021), and epidemic modeling. Must sharply differentiate ETAS-specific value (Omori-law decay kernel, GR magnitude distribution, background-triggered decomposition) from generic Hawkes applications.
MODERATE: Cancer branching process models exist (PLOS Comp Bio 2019, Nicholson et al.) using subcritical/supercritical concepts. ETAS must offer something these branching models do not.
MINOR: ctDNA 'magnitude' as Gutenberg-Richter analog requires operational definition — ctDNA levels are continuous, not discrete magnitude classes.
strengths: Zero papers connecting ETAS to oncology. Literature Scout confirmed 0 PubMed, 0 Semantic Scholar cross-field hits. DISJOINT confirmed at 0.90.
Fully specified mathematical framework with 5 named parameters (mu, K, alpha, c, p) and clear biological analogs for each.

Testable with EXISTING longitudinal ctDNA datasets — no wet-lab work needed. Multiple large-scale ctDNA monitoring studies (EP-SEASON, Nature Comms 2024-2025) provide empirical substrate. Median 8.7-month lead time between ctDNA detection and clinical recurrence provides temporal resolution for point process analysis.
ETAS branching ratio n provides binary clinical classifier: subcritical (n<1, dormancy self-limiting) vs supercritical (n>1, runaway recurrence cascade). Novel prognostic framework.
Seismology and clinical oncology both completely new to MAGELLAN — maximum frontier expansion.
structural_isomorphism validated in S011 (50% PASS+COND, avg composite 7.28) — known strategy performance.

key_evidence.popularity: Hawkes processes used in neuroscience and cell signaling but ETAS-specific framework has zero oncology presence. No review articles connecting seismology to oncology found.
key_evidence.vagueness: ETAS conditional intensity lambda(t|H_t) = mu + sum K*exp(alpha*(m_i-m_0))*(t-t_i+c)^(-p) — fully specified with 5 parameters. Each maps to a biological observable.
key_evidence.impossibility: No failed attempts found. Metastatic cascading (primary -> satellite -> tertiary metastases) provides biological substrate for self-exciting dynamics. ctDNA spike dynamics show temporal clustering consistent with point process structure.
key_evidence.local_optima: Seismology never used as Field A. Clinical oncology / recurrence never explored as Field C. Zero overlap with any prior session. Entirely novel territory.

evaluations.title: Mpemba Spectral Relaxation Theory Predicts Amyloid Aggregation Vulnerability
evaluations.field_a: Non-equilibrium statistical mechanics — Mpemba effect spectral theory
evaluations.field_c: Neurodegenerative protein biochemistry — amyloid aggregation selectivity
concerns: MODERATE: D_misfold '1000x slower than D_fold' claim needs specific literature grounding. If fabricated, becomes mechanism fabrication kill — a fatal pattern confirmed across S013, S014. Generator must cite primary source for this quantitative factor.

MODERATE: MSM datasets of sufficient quality may not exist for all proposed protein comparisons. Generator must specify exactly which amyloidogenic proteins (Ab42, alpha-synuclein, tau, TDP-43) have published MSMs with eigenmode data available for Mpemba index computation.

MODERATE: Extension from equilibrium MSMs (fixed temperature) to non-equilibrium Mpemba conditions (temperature quench) needs explicit mathematical treatment. Recent 2025 papers on non-normal Liouvillian dynamics provide tools but the bridge must be constructed explicitly.
MINOR: If only 2-3 amyloidogenic proteins have published MSMs, comparative Mpemba index across amyloidogenic vs non-amyloidogenic pairs may lack statistical power.
strengths: EXCEPTIONAL novelty: zero papers connecting Mpemba effect to any protein biology context. PubMed 0 hits. Semantic Scholar 0 cross-field hits. Highest disjointness confidence among candidates (0.95).

Shared mathematical formalism already exists in both fields independently: Markov state models with spectral decomposition are standard in both Mpemba theory and protein folding research (Pande, Noe groups). The bridge is natural, not forced.

Addresses explicitly acknowledged open question: why only ~30 of thousands of human proteins form pathological amyloids when thermodynamic stability alone does not predict propensity (PNAS 2020 reviews).

Mpemba physics experiencing rapid development (2024-2026): quantum Mpemba effect (Nature Comms 2024), non-normal dynamics (Entropy 2025), resource-theoretic unification (PRX March 2026). Field A is mature and actively expanding.
anomaly_hunting is the designated exploration slot (0 prior primary sessions). Provides critical empirical data for strategy performance with DISJOINT target.
Energy landscape compatibility confirmed: PNAS 2017 states systems with 'multiple local minima or metastable energy wells' show Mpemba effect — precisely the structure of protein folding landscapes.

key_evidence.popularity: Zero papers on Mpemba + protein biology. MSMs used in both fields but Mpemba eigenmode-overlap diagnostic absent from aggregation literature. Enzymatic Mpemba effect (2024 arXiv) focuses on enzyme kinetics, not aggregation.
key_evidence.vagueness: Named mathematical objects: Mpemba index (overlap integral with slowest eigenmode), spectral gap of MSM, non-normal Liouvillian. Concrete testable prediction: compare Mpemba index across amyloidogenic vs non-amyloidogenic protein pairs.
key_evidence.impossibility: PNAS 2017: 'multiple local minima or metastable energy wells' required — matches protein folding landscapes exactly. No structural barriers or counter-arguments found.
key_evidence.local_optima: Mpemba effect never appeared in any MAGELLAN session. Amyloid aggregation proposed but never selected (S001 deferred). anomaly_hunting has 1 prior primary session — exploration slot. Maximum frontier expansion.

evaluations.title: Anesthetic Lipid Raft Signaling Paradigm Explains Bacterial Sub-Lethal Antimicrobial Sensing
evaluations.field_a: Membrane biophysics / anesthesiology — PLD2 relocalization cascade
evaluations.field_c: Bacterial membrane biology — FMM-mediated AMP detection
concerns: CRITICAL: Eukaryotic GM1 lipid rafts and bacterial FMMs are structurally distinct systems. GM1 rafts are cholesterol-dependent, sphingolipid-rich ordered domains. Bacterial FMMs lack cholesterol entirely — they use polyisoprenoid lipids and hopanoids instead. PLD2 has no bacterial homolog. The bridge requires 'domain disruption triggers enzyme relocalization' to be a GENERAL PRINCIPLE independent of cholesterol, but this generality is assumed, not demonstrated. If the relocalization mechanism is cholesterol-dependent, it CANNOT transfer to cholesterol-free bacterial membranes.

CRITICAL: The proposed signal direction may be INVERTED. FloT scaffolds KinC for ACTIVATION — flotillin-defective mutants show REDUCED KinC activity and REDUCED sporulation (PMC 4591472). FMM disruption by AMPs would RELEASE KinC from the FloT scaffold, potentially INACTIVATING rather than activating the sporulation/biofilm response. The hypothesis proposes FMM disruption triggers KinC-mediated signaling, but the literature suggests the opposite: FMM integrity is REQUIRED for KinC signaling. This is a potential structural inversion kill.

MODERATE: B. subtilis FloT-KinC-Spo0A pathway is well-characterized for SURFACTIN-mediated biofilm induction (Lopez lab). The claim is that AMPs trigger the same pathway, but surfactin is self-produced and recognized specifically. How would FMM disruption by diverse AMPs differ from disruption by surfactin, detergents, or temperature stress? Specificity problem unresolved.

MODERATE: The anesthesia PLD2 paradigm was revived only in 2020 (Hansen PNAS) and remains debated — some researchers maintain direct protein-binding models. Transferring a paradigm that is itself contested increases vulnerability to 'alternative explanation' attacks from the Critic.
MODERATE: failed_paradigm_recycling has zero primary sessions — no empirical performance baseline. Combined with two critical structural concerns, risk is elevated.
strengths: Genuinely novel connection: zero papers linking anesthesia PLD2/GM1 mechanism to bacterial FMMs. Literature Scout confirmed 0 cross-field hits. DISJOINT at 0.93.

Both systems share the same abstract principle: ordered membrane domain disruption as a signaling trigger rather than a damage indicator. This is a transferable concept if the molecular details can be grounded.

The anesthesia PLD2 cascade (Hansen 2020 PNAS) provides a COMPLETE, experimentally validated signaling pathway with 4 steps: GM1 disruption -> PLD2 relocalization -> PA production -> TREK-1 activation. Complete template pathways are rare.
Addresses genuine open question: how bacteria detect sub-lethal AMPs before membrane damage occurs (Garcia-Fernandez 2017 Cell shows FMM disruption affects resistance but sensing mechanism unknown).
Failed paradigm recycling is a high-creativity strategy: applying a paradigm abandoned in one field to a problem in a completely different field. Novel strategy provides exploration slot data.
Translational potential: FMM-stabilizing compounds as antibiotic adjuvants that block anticipatory resistance is a novel drug target concept.

key_evidence.popularity: Zero papers linking PLD2/GM1 anesthesia mechanism to bacterial FMMs. Anesthesia and bacterial FMM literatures are completely disjoint — confirmed by Literature Scout.
key_evidence.vagueness: Named proteins (PLD2, FloT, KinC, Spo0A), named lipids (PA, GM1, polyisoprenoids), specific model system (B. subtilis). Proposed experiments (zaragozic acid FMM disruption). However, the PLD2-to-FloT mapping is analogical — no sequence or functional homology.
key_evidence.impossibility: GM1 raft and bacterial FMM structural incompatibility is the primary concern. Cholesterol dependence of GM1 domains may make the PLD2 relocalization mechanism non-transferable. FloT-KinC signal direction may be inverted (disruption = inactivation, not activation). These two issues together score this axis at 5/10.
key_evidence.local_optima: Membrane biophysics/anesthesiology never used as Field A. Bacterial membrane biology never explored as Field C. failed_paradigm_recycling is a new strategy. Good frontier expansion, but not maximum (some overlap with S013's bacterial biology Field C).

best_target.title: Mpemba Spectral Relaxation Theory Predicts Amyloid Aggregation Vulnerability
best_target.reason: Highest composite score. Exceptional novelty (zero papers, 0.95 disjointness). Shared MSM formalism as natural bridge. Addresses genuine open question. Exploration slot provides strategy performance data for anomaly_hunting.

second_target.title: Earthquake ETAS Self-Exciting Point Process Models Tumor Recurrence Dynamics
second_target.reason: Strong novelty and specificity with fully specified mathematical framework. Entirely new territory for MAGELLAN. ctDNA datasets provide existing empirical substrate. Highest impact potential (8/10). Self-exciting assumption is the main biological risk.

third_target.title: Anesthetic Lipid Raft Signaling Paradigm Explains Bacterial Sub-Lethal Antimicrobial Sensing
third_target.reason: Two critical structural concerns lower the score: (1) cholesterol-dependent GM1 raft mechanisms may not transfer to cholesterol-free bacterial membranes, (2) FloT-KinC signal direction may be inverted — FMM disruption inactivates rather than activates KinC. Creative connection but structural foundation needs resolution.

summary.pipeline_recommendation: PROCEED — all targets >= 5.0. Target 3 flagged CAUTION.
recommended_selection_order: Mpemba Spectral Relaxation Theory Predicts Amyloid Aggregation Vulnerability
Earthquake ETAS Self-Exciting Point Process Models Tumor Recurrence Dynamics
Anesthetic Lipid Raft Signaling Paradigm Explains Bacterial Sub-Lethal Antimicrobial Sensing
